hero.headline: Brandon Oechsner

Background spans embedded systems, DSP, CAD/fabrication, and hands-on electrical work from an apprenticeship background.

experience.description: Supporting aerospace engineering operations at one of the world's leading jet engine manufacturers. Hands-on exposure to precision manufacturing processes, engineering documentation, and the quality systems behind flight-critical components.

Designing and deploying robotic process automation solutions that eliminate manual, repetitive workflows across healthcare operations. Identifying automation opportunities, building bots, and measuring process efficiency gains in a live clinical environment.

Coursework spanning DSP, embedded systems, circuit analysis, controls, and industrial automation. Lab work includes FIR/IIR filter implementation on TMS320C6748 DSP hardware, PLC ladder logic programming, and pneumatic systems. Pre-college electrical apprenticeship background provides rare hands-on field experience alongside academic study.

projects.title: 6-DOF Arduino Robotic Arm
projects.description: 6-degree-of-freedom robotic arm built from scratch — Arduino UNO driving a PCA9685 servo shield, MG996R and SG90 servos at each joint, and fully custom 3D-printed structural components designed in Onshape for the Bambu Lab P1S.
projects.body:
This project started as an exercise in mechanical design and grew into a full embedded systems build. Every structural component was designed parametrically in Onshape so dimensions could be adjusted without rebuilding the assembly from scratch. The arm was sliced and printed in PETG on the Bambu Lab P1S for its strength and dimensional accuracy.

The control system runs on an Arduino UNO driving a PCA9685 16-channel PWM servo shield, which handles all six joints without saturating the Arduino's PWM outputs. MG996R high-torque servos handle the shoulder and elbow joints while lighter SG90s handle the wrist and gripper. Joint angles are mapped from potentiometer inputs, allowing real-time manual control. The next phase involves adding inverse kinematics so the end effector can be positioned by target coordinates rather than individual joint angles.
highlights: 6-DOF kinematic chain — shoulder, elbow, wrist rotation, wrist pitch, gripper
PCA9685 PWM servo shield offloads all 16 servo channels from the Arduino
MG996R high-torque servos at load-bearing joints, SG90s at wrist and gripper
Fully parametric Onshape assembly — adjust one variable, everything updates
PETG printed on Bambu Lab P1S for strength and dimensional accuracy
Inverse kinematics implementation planned for next revision

projects.title: Treadmill Motor & Drive Teardown
projects.description: Disassembled a commercial treadmill to salvage the DC drive motor, PWM motor controller, and mechanical subsystems. Documented component specs and catalogued each part for reuse in future builds.
projects.body:
Rather than discarding a broken commercial treadmill, I disassembled it systematically to recover high-value components. The DC treadmill motor — typically a 2–3 HP permanent magnet motor — is ideal for reuse in a lathe, belt grinder, or variable-speed drive project because the existing PWM motor controller already provides smooth, variable-speed control.

The teardown involved identifying and bench-testing the motor controller board, mapping the wiring harness, and recording torque and speed ratings from the motor nameplate. The process required understanding how the controller interfaces with the speed sensor and safety key circuit before isolating the motor for standalone operation.
highlights: Recovered 2+ HP DC permanent magnet motor suitable for lathe or belt grinder build
Mapped and isolated the PWM motor controller for standalone reuse
Documented wiring harness — speed sensor, safety key, and throttle interfaces
Catalogued all mechanical hardware: rollers, belt, deck, frame extrusions
Demonstrates component-level understanding over black-box thinking

projects.title: FIR / IIR Filter Implementation
projects.description: Designed and deployed FIR and IIR digital filters in MATLAB and on a TMS320C6748 DSP board via Code Composer Studio. Applications include echo generation, audio signal shaping, and filter frequency analysis.
projects.body:
For my DSP coursework I designed, simulated, and deployed both FIR and IIR digital filters — first in MATLAB to verify frequency response, then compiled and flashed onto the TMS320C6748 fixed-point DSP board via Code Composer Studio.

Applications included a bandpass filter for audio shaping, an echo effect implemented with a feedback delay line, and a notch filter to eliminate a simulated 60 Hz power-line hum. Comparing filter types revealed the tradeoff clearly: FIR filters gave linear phase response at the cost of a higher tap count, while IIR filters achieved sharper roll-off with fewer coefficients but introduced phase nonlinearity.
highlights: FIR bandpass and notch filters designed with windowing method in MATLAB
IIR Butterworth filter for sharper roll-off with lower coefficient count
Echo effect via feedback delay line — adjustable delay and decay
Real-time implementation on TMS320C6748 via Code Composer Studio
Frequency response measured and compared against MATLAB simulation

projects.title: Marquise Diamond Engagement Ring
projects.description: Parametric engagement ring design in Onshape featuring a custom marquise diamond setting with a flower petal prong aesthetic. Constructed using loft-based prong geometry with guide curves.
projects.body:
A personal project — designing an engagement ring from scratch in Onshape rather than buying one off the shelf. The marquise-cut stone setting required building each prong as a lofted solid between two sketch profiles, with guide curves controlling the organic petal shape as the prong tapers toward the tip.

The shank is a simple half-round profile swept along an elliptical path sized to the target finger measurement. Everything is fully parametric: stone size, prong count, shank width, and ring size are all driven by variables at the top of the feature tree. The design was printed in resin to check fit and proportion before any metal work.
highlights: Fully parametric — stone size, prong count, and ring size all variable-driven
Loft-based prong geometry with guide curves for organic petal taper
Elliptical shank sweep sized to target finger measurement
Resin prototype printed to verify fit and proportion
Designed entirely in Onshape with no external plugins
